items.line_en: So, today we are going to look at the past perfect tense.

coach.say: So, today we are going to look at the past perfect tense.
coach.hint: Chunk it: So, / today we are going to look at the past perfect tense.

items.line_en: We use the past perfect to talk about an action that finished before another action in the

coach.say: We use the past perfect to talk about an action that finished before another action in the
coach.hint: Chunk it: We use the past perfect to talk about an action that finished before another action in the

items.line_en: past.

coach.say: past.
coach.hint: Chunk it: past.

items.line_en: Subject plus had plus past participle.

coach.say: Subject plus had plus past participle.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Subject plus had plus past participle.

items.line_en: She had finished her work.

coach.say: She had finished her work.
coach.hint: Chunk it: She had finished her work.

items.line_en: This sentence, they had left the house is an example of the past perfect tense.

coach.say: This sentence, they had left the house is an example of the past perfect tense.
coach.hint: Chunk it: This sentence, / they had left the house is an example of the past perfect tense.

items.line_en: I had finished my work.

coach.say: I had finished my work.
coach.hint: Chunk it: I had finished my work.

items.line_en: When we use the past perfect, it shows one action was finished when a second one interrupted

coach.say: When we use the past perfect, it shows one action was finished when a second one interrupted
coach.hint: Chunk it: When we use the past perfect, / it shows one action was finished when a second one interrupted

items.line_en: it.

coach.say: it.
coach.hint: Chunk it: it.

items.line_en: We use the past perfect to describe something that happened before another action in the past,

coach.say: We use the past perfect to describe something that happened before another action in the past,
coach.hint: Chunk it: We use the past perfect to describe something that happened before another action in the past,

items.line_en: especially when we use when to show an interruption.

coach.say: especially when we use when to show an interruption.
coach.hint: Chunk it: especially when we use when / to show an interruption.

items.line_en: Here's another example, using when.

coach.say: Here's another example, using when.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Here's another example, / using when.

items.line_en: When the rain stops, we can go outside and play.

coach.say: When the rain stops, we can go outside and play.
coach.hint: Chunk it: When the rain stops, / we can go outside and play.

items.line_en: He had fallen asleep when the phone rang.

coach.say: He had fallen asleep when the phone rang.
coach.hint: Chunk it: He had fallen asleep when / the phone rang.

items.line_en: We used the past perfect to show which action happened first.

coach.say: We used the past perfect to show which action happened first.
coach.hint: Chunk it: We used the past perfect to show which action happened first.

items.line_en: For example, I had finished my homework before I went to bed.

coach.say: For example, I had finished my homework before I went to bed.
coach.hint: Chunk it: For example, / I had finished my homework before I went to bed.

items.line_en: The word before means at an earlier time.

coach.say: The word before means at an earlier time.
coach.hint: Chunk it: The word before means at an earlier time.

items.line_en: Make sure you brush your teeth before you go to bed.

coach.say: Make sure you brush your teeth before you go to bed.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Make sure you brush your teeth before you go to bed.

items.line_en: Before means happening earlier in time.

coach.say: Before means happening earlier in time.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Before means happening earlier in time.

items.line_en: For example, you wash your hands before you eat.

coach.say: For example, you wash your hands before you eat.
coach.hint: Chunk it: For example, / you wash your hands before you eat.

items.line_en: Here is another example.

coach.say: Here is another example.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Here is another example.

items.line_en: Remember to finish your homework before you come to class tomorrow.

coach.say: Remember to finish your homework before you come to class tomorrow.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Remember to finish your homework before you come to class tomorrow.

items.line_en: The rule for the negative past perfect is straightforward.

coach.say: The rule for the negative past perfect is straightforward.
coach.hint: Chunk it: The rule for the negative past perfect is straightforward.

items.line_en: We use had not or hadn't followed by the past participle.

coach.say: We use had not or hadn't followed by the past participle.
coach.hint: Chunk it: We use had not or / hadn't followed by the past participle.

items.line_en: To make a negative sentence, we add not after had.

coach.say: To make a negative sentence, we add not after had.
coach.hint: Chunk it: To make a negative sentence, / we add not after had.

items.line_en: I had not seen the movie.

coach.say: I had not seen the movie.
coach.hint: Chunk it: I had not seen the movie.

items.line_en: To ask a question in the past perfect tense, we start with had, then the subject

coach.say: To ask a question in the past perfect tense, we start with had, then the subject
coach.hint: Chunk it: To ask a question in the past perfect tense, / we start with had, / then the subject

items.line_en: and finally the past participle.

coach.say: and finally the past participle.
coach.hint: Chunk it: and finally the past participle.

items.line_en: For questions, had comes before the subject.

coach.say: For questions, had comes before the subject.
coach.hint: Chunk it: For questions, / had comes before the subject.

items.line_en: Had you finished your work?

coach.say: Had you finished your work?
coach.hint: Chunk it: Had you finished your work?

items.line_en: Had they left before sunset?

coach.say: Had they left before sunset?
coach.hint: Chunk it: Had they left before sunset?

items.line_en: All right class, who can give me an example of a past perfect sentence?

coach.say: All right class, who can give me an example of a past perfect sentence?
coach.hint: Chunk it: All right class, / who can give me an example of a past perfect sentence?

items.line_en: Think about the structure we just discussed.

coach.say: Think about the structure we just discussed.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Think about the structure we just discussed.

items.line_en: Today we're looking at common mistakes.

coach.say: Today we're looking at common mistakes.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Today we're looking at common mistakes.

items.line_en: Remember, when using the past perfect, you should never use did.

coach.say: Remember, when using the past perfect, you should never use did.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Remember, / when using the past perfect, / you should never use did.

items.line_en: Structure, positive, negative, and question forms.

coach.say: Structure, positive, negative, and question forms.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Structure, / positive, / negative, / and question forms.

items.line_en: Great job!

coach.say: Great job!
coach.hint: Chunk it: Great job!

items.line_en: Today you learned the past perfect tense.

coach.say: Today you learned the past perfect tense.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Today you learned the past perfect tense.
